key_insight: CANN-EUCLID discovers sparse, interpretable hyperelastic laws directly from full-field displacement and force data, without local stress measurements or a prescribed constitutive form.
engineering_relevance: This enables constitutive model identification from a single heterogeneous test, which is especially valuable for soft biological tissues where repeated loading and stress supervision are difficult.
method: Combine constitutive neural networks with the EUCLID stress-unsupervised full-field framework.
Minimize equilibrium imbalance using displacement fields and reaction forces.
Use sparsity-promoting regularization to select a compact set of active constitutive terms.
evidence_signal: On isotropic and anisotropic benchmark problems, the method recovered the correct terms nearly exactly when the true law was in the basis and produced accurate forward finite-element predictions.
watch_item: Generalization can fail if key deformation regimes, such as strain-stiffening, are not sufficiently sampled, leading to large extrapolation errors.
paper_title: CANN-EUCLID: unsupervised constitutive artificial neural network model discovery from full-field data
